LLoom
HomeFeaturesDocsDownloadChangelog

Documentation

Everything you need to use Loom effectively.

Getting Started

What is Loom?
Loom is a local-first AI coding agent CLI. It connects to Ollama, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Anthropic, Gemini, and Groq. You type natural language prompts and Loom routes them to the best model, reads/writes files, runs shell commands, and more.
Quick start
Install with `npm install -g loom-agent`, then run `loom` in any project directory. Type a prompt like "explain the codebase" or "find the auth bug". Loom will route your task, analyze code, and return results.
Requirements
Node.js 18.17+ recommended. For local mode, install Ollama and pull a model (e.g., `ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:7b`). For cloud providers, set API keys via .env or config.

Usage

How do I run Loom?
Type `loom` for interactive mode, or `loom <prompt>` (`loom run <prompt>`) for one-shot. Use `loom --help` to see all commands.
Agent modes
Loom has 6 modes: plan, build, review, debug, research, test. Switch with `/plan`, `/build`, etc. inside the REPL, or type a prompt and let auto-routing decide.
Slash commands
Inside the REPL, type `/help` to see all commands. Key ones: `/clear`, `/providers`, `/models`, `/memory`, `/sessions`, `/index`, `/config`, `/doctor`.
Configuration
Create a `.loomrc.json` in your project root, or run `loom init` to generate one. See the GitHub repo for a full config reference.

Providers

Which providers are supported?
Ollama (local), OpenAI, OpenRouter, Anthropic, Gemini, and Groq. Configure API keys in `.env` or `.loomrc.json`.
How do I use a local model?
Install Ollama, pull a model (`ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:7b`), and set it as default in `.loomrc.json`. Run `loom --local` to force local inference.
Auto-routing
Loom analyzes each prompt and routes to the best model: coding tasks to code models, research to general models, etc. Configure routing in `.loomrc.json`.

Configuration Reference

.loomrc.json
The config file supports: providers, providerEndpoints, aliases, agent (maxIterations, temperature), safety (blockedCommands, requireConfirmForShell), models (coding, reasoning, general, local, fallback), routing (defaultMode, fallbackToLocal), tools, verification, mcpServers.
Environment variables
Set `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in `.env` at your project root or home directory.
Safety settings
By default, Loom requires confirmation for shell commands. You can disable this, block specific commands (rm -rf /, mkfs, etc.), or enable sandbox mode via config.

API & Extensibility

Plugin system
Place `.js` or `.mjs` files in `.loom/plugins/` in your project or `~/.loom/plugins/`. Each plugin exports a default function that receives a tool registry. See the GitHub repo for examples.
MCP support
Loom supports Model Context Protocol servers. Configure them in `.loomrc.json` under `mcpServers`. Use `loom mcp --help` for CLI commands.
Programmatic API
Import the Loom agent programmatically: `import { Agent } from 'loom-agent'`. See the source code for the full API.

More questions? Open an issue

Loom— Local-first AI coding agent
